meta.title: Global mineral, rare-earth & nuclear asset registry
meta.note: Seed dataset. Locations are approximate site centroids; production and capacity figures are approximate latest public values and require verification against operator disclosures before publication.

sites.name: Escondida
sites.note: Atacama porphyry complex; roughly 5% of global mined copper

sites.name: Collahuasi
sites.note: High-altitude porphyry; consistent top-5 producer

sites.name: El Teniente
sites.note: World's largest underground copper mine

sites.name: Chuquicamata
sites.note: Century-old open pit converting to underground

sites.name: Grasberg
sites.note: Production suspended after the Sept 2025 landslide; restart phased

sites.name: Kamoa-Kakula
sites.note: Highest-grade major copper complex; 2025 flooding halted ramp-up

sites.name: Tenke Fungurume
sites.note: Copper-cobalt giant; now the #2 copper mine globally

sites.name: Oyu Tolgoi
sites.note: Underground block cave in the Gobi; top-4 by 2030

sites.name: Morenci
sites.note: Largest North American copper producer

sites.name: Cerro Verde
sites.note: Concentrator among the world's largest

sites.name: Antamina
sites.note: High-Andes polymetallic skarn

sites.name: Las Bambas
sites.note: Output episodically hit by community blockades

sites.name: Carajás (Serra Norte)
sites.note: World's largest iron ore mine; highest-grade major system

sites.name: Mount Whaleback
sites.note: Anchor of the Pilbara iron province

sites.name: Hamersley (Tom Price)
sites.note: Rio's original Pilbara mine, 1966

sites.name: Simandou
sites.note: Largest untapped high-grade iron deposit; first ore 2025-26

sites.name: Kiruna
sites.note: World's largest underground iron mine; adjacent Per Geijer REE resource is Europe's largest

sites.name: Norilsk
sites.note: Arctic polymetallic complex; world's top palladium source

sites.name: Weda Bay
sites.figure: world's largest Ni mine by output
sites.note: Halmahera laterite feeding Indonesia's NPI/HPAL boom

sites.name: Sorowako
sites.note: Long-life laterite operation, Sulawesi

sites.name: Mutanda
sites.note: Restarted 2022 after care & maintenance; DRC supplies ~70% of cobalt

sites.name: Greenbushes
sites.note: Highest-grade hard-rock lithium mine on earth

sites.name: Salar de Atacama
sites.note: World's richest lithium brine; half of global brine output

sites.name: Cauchari-Olaroz
sites.note: Lithium Triangle's largest new brine project

sites.name: Thacker Pass
sites.note: Largest known US lithium resource; DOE-backed

sites.name: Muruntau
sites.note: Kyzylkum open pit; largest gold mine by output and pit scale

sites.name: Olimpiada
sites.note: Russia's biggest gold operation

sites.name: Carlin Trend (NGM)
sites.note: The most productive gold district in the western hemisphere

sites.name: Boddington
sites.note: Australia's largest gold mine

sites.name: Pueblo Viejo
sites.note: Caribbean's largest gold operation

sites.name: Mogalakwena
sites.note: World's largest open-pit PGM mine, Bushveld Complex

sites.name: Fresnillo
sites.note: World's premier primary silver district

sites.name: Cannington
sites.note: Among the largest single silver-lead mines

sites.name: Red Dog
sites.note: Arctic Alaska; world's largest zinc mine

sites.name: Rampura Agucha
sites.note: World's largest underground zinc operation

sites.name: Weipa
sites.note: One of the largest bauxite complexes globally

sites.name: Sangaredi (CBG)
sites.note: Guinea holds the world's largest bauxite reserves

sites.name: Bayan Obo
sites.note: World's dominant REE source; >80% of China's reserves

sites.name: Mountain Pass
sites.note: The only US rare-earth mine; magnetics buildout underway

sites.name: Mount Weld
sites.note: Richest known REE orebody outside China

sites.name: Lovozero
sites.note: Kola Peninsula Arctic REE district

sites.name: Nolans Bore
sites.note: Fully-permitted Australian NdPr project

sites.name: Tanbreez (Kringlerne)
sites.note: Environmental permit cleared 2026; drilling underway; ore targeted 2028-29 — the West's largest heavy-REE project

sites.name: Kuannersuit (Kvanefjeld)
sites.note: Halted by Greenland's 2021 uranium ban (>100 ppm U)

sites.name: Citronen Fjord
sites.note: One of the northernmost mineral projects on earth

sites.name: Isua
sites.note: Ice-margin iron project, 150 km NE of Nuuk

sites.name: Malmbjerg
sites.note: East Greenland climax-type moly deposit

sites.name: Cigar Lake
sites.note: World's highest-grade uranium mine, Athabasca Basin

sites.name: McArthur River
sites.note: Largest high-grade uranium deposit; restarted 2022

sites.name: Olympic Dam
sites.note: Polymetallic giant: copper, uranium, gold, silver

sites.name: Husab
sites.note: China-backed; among top-3 uranium mines

sites.name: Inkai
sites.note: Kazakhstan supplies ~40% of world uranium

sites.name: Arlit (SOMAIR)
sites.note: Operations disrupted since the 2023 coup; ownership contested

sites.name: Kashiwazaki-Kariwa
sites.note: Idled post-2011; restart approvals in progress

sites.name: Hanul
sites.note: World's largest operating nuclear site by capacity

sites.name: Kori
sites.note: One of the largest operating stations globally

sites.name: Bruce
sites.note: Western hemisphere's largest operating plant

sites.name: Zaporizhzhia
sites.note: In cold shutdown under military occupation

sites.name: Gravelines
sites.note: Largest plant in Western Europe; two EPR2 units planned

sites.name: Barakah
sites.note: First Arab nuclear plant; full fleet online 2024

sites.name: Palo Verde
sites.note: Largest US generator of any kind, most years

sites.name: Vogtle
sites.note: First new US reactors in a generation (2023-24)

sites.name: Taishan
sites.note: First EPRs in commercial operation

sites.name: Olkiluoto
sites.note: Also hosts Onkalo, the world's first deep geological repository

sites.name: Hinkley Point C
sites.note: First new UK nuclear in 30 years

sites.name: Akkuyu
sites.note: Turkey's first nuclear plant

sites.name: Darlington New Nuclear
sites.note: First grid-scale SMR build in the West
